attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Partial verification only: unable to open/parse the .xlsx to confirm presence of executable formulas and row-level citations; relied on process_disposition.md assertions and recalculation of the simple action arithmetic presented.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a parseable backup (CSV or text export of key formula cells) or ensure the workbook is viewable in this environment (provide exported formula-summary sheet). In the workbook, include explicit cell references (sheet!A1 style) cited in the markdown to make row-level traceability unambiguous. Provide an embedded short table mapping each numeric used to the exact embedded-packet field/row cited.
rubric_item_scores.notes: The submitted process_disposition.md documents batch inputs, specs, action impacts, costs, simulation traces, and a final decision table. Numerical simulations shown in the markdown are internally consistent with the stated action impacts and costs: e.g., B2 initial PDI 0.31 -> reprocess reduces PDI by 0.04 to 0.27 (still >0.25); combined dia(+0.02)/reprocess(-0.04) yields 0.29; B3 initial ethanol 0.36 -> dia -0.12 = 0.24 and combined dia+reprocess yields encap 94.1, size 90, PDI 0.22, ethanol 0.24 which meets specs; combined action cost computed as 2 * 68,000 = 136,000 USD. The markdown claims workbook sheets with formula-based checks and a sensitivity analysis with stated perturbations. Confidence is slightly reduced because the XLSX could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so I verified arithmetic and logic from the readable markdown but could not inspect cell-level formulas or row-level citations inside the workbook.

Ensure the spreadsheet contains executable formulas (not static values), row-level citations to the embedded packet, and that action simulations and sensitivity analyses are implemented and documented. Fix the script syntax error shown in stderr and re-run to produce the required deliverables; explicitly declare any unresolved or ambiguous input values rather than inventing numbers.
